name: Commerce Village
meaning: The Gold Traders — the merchant guild who connected all of West Africa through trans-Saharan trade
history: The Wangara were a guild of Mandé-speaking merchants who controlled the trans-Saharan gold trade for 600 years. At the peak of the Mali Empire they moved more gold than any civilisation on earth. Their trade network stretched from Senegal to the Niger Bend. When Mansa Musa made his 1324 pilgrimage to Mecca with 60,000 people and tonnes of gold, it was Wangara trade networks that built that wealth. They were Africa's first merchant bankers, first logistics network, first commodity traders.
guardianDesc: Anansi the Spider is the Akan deity of commerce and cunning intelligence from Ghana. He wove the first trade web across West Africa — outwitting gods to give stories and commerce to humanity. Every market stall carries his invisible thread.
origin: Mandé Gold-Trading Guild · Mali Empire · c. 700–1600 CE
tagline: The market that feeds the nation. Every trade, every stall, every deal.

roles.name: Market Vendor / Mama Put Seller
roles.desc: Physical market stall, roadside food seller, neighbourhood market trader across all 54 nations

roles.name: Import / Export Agent
roles.desc: Cross-border trade, customs clearing, freight forwarding, container logistics

roles.name: Mobile Money & POS Agent
roles.desc: M-Pesa agent, POS operator, airtime vendor, cash transfer service

roles.name: Wholesaler / Bulk Distributor
roles.desc: Bulk commodity trading, warehouse operations, supply to retailers

roles.name: E-Commerce & Social Seller
roles.desc: Jumia/Konga seller, TikTok shop, Instagram commerce, WhatsApp business

roles.name: Bureau de Change / Forex Trader
roles.desc: Currency exchange, international remittance, forex trading

roles.name: Second-Hand Goods Dealer
roles.desc: Bend-down boutique, Gikomba trader, mitumba clothing, refurbished electronics

roles.name: Auctioneer / Commodity Broker
roles.desc: Livestock auctioneer, grain broker, commodity exchange trader

name: Agriculture Village
meaning: The Black Land — named for the rich Nile silt that fed an empire of five million people for three thousand years
history: Kemet is what Ancient Egypt called itself — the black fertile soil of the Nile flood. Egypt developed the first systematic irrigation, the first crop calendars, the first granary management systems. The Edwin Smith Papyrus (1600 BC), the world's oldest agricultural text, was written here. Kemet was the breadbasket of the ancient world, exporting grain to Greece, Rome, and the Mediterranean.
guardianDesc: Osiris is the Ancient Egyptian god of agriculture, death, and resurrection. He is killed each year and rises again — like every harvest. He taught humanity to farm, to wait for the season, and to trust the soil. The Nile floods in his name.
origin: Ancient Egyptian name for Egypt · The Black Land · c. 3100 BCE
tagline: The soil that feeds 1.4 billion souls.

roles.name: Crop Farmer
roles.desc: Smallholder and commercial farmer — maize, cassava, yam, sorghum, millet, rice, cocoa, coffee, tea

roles.name: Pastoralist / Livestock Keeper
roles.desc: Cattle herder, goat farmer, poultry farmer, dairy farmer, pig farmer, camel herder

roles.name: Artisanal Fisher / Aquaculture
roles.desc: Lake Victoria fisher, Atlantic coastal fisherman, fish farmer, shrimp farmer

roles.name: Agro-Processor
roles.desc: Palm oil processor, cassava flour miller, groundnut paste maker, dairy processor

roles.name: Irrigation & Water Engineer
roles.desc: Borehole driller, canal builder, drip irrigation installer, dam maintenance

roles.name: Seed Bank Guardian / Agronomist
roles.desc: Heritage seed keeper, soil scientist, crop geneticist, agricultural extension officer

roles.name: Rain Watcher / Weather Monitor
roles.desc: Community climate observer, flood early warning officer, drought response coordinator

roles.name: Farm-to-Market Coordinator
roles.desc: Produce aggregator, cold chain manager, rural transport coordinator

name: Health Village
meaning: The Pure Place — sacred healing sanctuaries where Imhotep, the world's first named physician, practiced medicine
history: The Wabet were sacred purification chambers of ancient Egyptian temples where healing priests practiced. The Edwin Smith Papyrus (1600 BC) describes 48 medical cases including brain surgery. Imhotep (2650 BC) was the world's first named physician — later deified as a god of medicine. Africa invented medicine 4,600 years before the Hippocratic oath.
guardianDesc: Sekhmet is the Ancient Egyptian lioness goddess of medicine and surgery. She causes disease and cures it — the divine paradox of healing. Her temple complexes were the world's first hospitals. She is the original healer, patron of all who fight illness with knowledge.
origin: Sacred healing chamber in every major Egyptian temple · c. 2500 BCE
tagline: Where bodies are healed and lives are saved.

roles.name: Medical Doctor / Physician
roles.desc: GP, specialist surgeon, gynaecologist, paediatrician, psychiatrist, oncologist

roles.name: Nurse / Birth Attendant
roles.desc: Registered nurse, enrolled nurse, auxiliary nurse, skilled birth attendant

roles.name: Traditional Healer / Herbalist
roles.desc: Sangoma, Babalawo, Nganga, Dibia, Fetish priest — licensed practitioners

roles.name: Community Health Worker
roles.desc: Village health volunteer, vaccination outreach, malaria net distributor, HIV counsellor

roles.name: Pharmacist / Drug Dispenser
roles.desc: Hospital pharmacist, community pharmacy owner, drug distribution officer

roles.name: Mental Health Practitioner
roles.desc: Clinical psychologist, trauma counsellor, substance abuse counsellor

roles.name: Lab Scientist / Diagnostician
roles.desc: Medical laboratory scientist, pathologist, radiology technician

roles.name: Midwife / Certified Birth Attendant
roles.desc: Certified midwife, traditional birth attendant, postnatal care specialist

name: Education Village
meaning: The University on the Hill — one of the world's first universities with 25,000 students and 700,000 manuscripts
history: The University of Sankore in Timbuktu predates Oxford University by 200 years. At its peak it had 25,000 students studying law, medicine, mathematics, astronomy, and theology. Timbuktu's private libraries held 700,000 manuscripts — more books than anywhere in the medieval world. When colonisers called Africa 'the dark continent,' Sankore was the counter-evidence.
guardianDesc: Thoth is the Ancient Egyptian god of knowledge, writing, and wisdom. He invented writing and gave it to humanity. The scribe of the gods — the divine librarian who maintains all records of existence. All who transmit knowledge work under his gaze.
origin: Great mosque-university of Timbuktu · founded 989 CE · 25,000 students
tagline: The library that rewrites the lie that Africa had no knowledge.

roles.name: Classroom Teacher
roles.desc: Primary, secondary, community education across all 54 African nations

roles.name: University Lecturer
roles.desc: Higher education, research, publication, academic leadership

roles.name: Community Tutor
roles.desc: Private tutoring, adult literacy, skills coaching

roles.name: School Administrator
roles.desc: School management, staff coordination, community liaison

roles.name: Vocational Trainer
roles.desc: Trade skills, artisan training, apprenticeship coordination

roles.name: Language Keeper
roles.desc: African language instruction, oral tradition preservation

roles.name: EdTech Developer
roles.desc: Digital learning tools, e-learning platforms, educational apps

roles.name: Cultural Historian
roles.desc: African history research, manuscript preservation, publication

name: Arts Village
meaning: The First Sculptors — oldest sub-Saharan sculpture tradition and first iron-smelters in West Africa
history: The Nok culture flourished in central Nigeria for over 2,000 years. Their terracotta sculptures show remarkable sophistication predating all other West African sculpture by 1,000 years. The Nok were also the first iron-smelters in sub-Saharan Africa. Their tradition influenced all later West African art: the Ife bronzes, the Benin bronzes, the Nupe brasswork. Arts Village draws from 3,500 years of unbroken African creative tradition.
guardianDesc: Oya is the Yoruba goddess of storms, transformation, and creative power. She tears down old forms to make space for new creation. Patron of all artists who destroy convention to build something new. She governs all creative destruction.
origin: Oldest sub-Saharan African civilisation · First terracotta sculpture · c. 1500 BCE
tagline: Every artist carries a 3,500-year creative lineage.

roles.name: Musician / Griot
roles.desc: Music production, live performance, recording, digital distribution

roles.name: Visual Artist
roles.desc: Painting, sculpture, digital art, NFTs, gallery representation

roles.name: Author / Poet
roles.desc: Storytelling, poetry, journalism, narrative, publishing

roles.name: Filmmaker
roles.desc: Documentary, short film, feature, video production

roles.name: Dancer / Performer
roles.desc: Dance, theatre, live performance, festival coordination

roles.name: Master Artisan
roles.desc: Kente weaving, adire dyeing, beadwork, pottery, woodcarving

roles.name: Fashion Designer
roles.desc: African fashion design, kente, adinkra, ankara patterns

roles.name: Cultural Educator
roles.desc: Cultural preservation, arts education, heritage programming

name: Builders Village
meaning: The City of Pyramids — built 200+ pyramids and conquered Egypt as the 25th Dynasty
history: The Kingdom of Kush at Meroë built more pyramids than all of ancient Egypt combined — over 200 pyramids. The Kush Empire conquered and ruled Egypt for 80 years as the 25th Dynasty. Their iron-smelting technology was so advanced that metal tools spread across all of sub-Saharan Africa from Meroë. The Meroitic alphabet they created remains only partially translated.
guardianDesc: Dedwen is the Ancient Nubian god of prosperity and construction. The first patron of builders. He provides the incense burned when a new structure is consecrated. The Nubian pyramids were raised in his name.
origin: Capital of Kingdom of Kush · 200+ pyramids · Sudan · c. 800 BCE
tagline: Africa built more pyramids than Egypt. Meroë is the proof.

roles.name: Architect
roles.desc: Building design, structural planning, project leadership

roles.name: Civil Engineer
roles.desc: Infrastructure design, roads, bridges, drainage, project management

roles.name: Mason / Bricklayer

roles.name: Electrician

roles.name: Plumber

roles.name: Carpenter / Welder

roles.name: Heavy Machinery Operator
roles.desc: Excavator, crane, bulldozer, compactor operations

name: Energy Village
nationFull: Congo DRC · Congo River Basin · Inga Falls
meaning: The Eternal Falls — the Congo River carries 40,000 cubic metres per second, the world's greatest untapped power source
history: The Congo River at Inga Falls could generate 100,000 megawatts — enough to power all of Africa and export to Europe. The Congo Basin rainforest is the world's second lung. While 600 million Africans have no reliable electricity, the solution sits in Africa's heart. Energy Village exists to build the networks that will change that calculation.
guardianDesc: Nzambi Mpungu is the supreme creator of the Kongo people — the force that set the universe in motion. The original energy. The first power before time. The Congo River is Nzambi's spine.
origin: Inga Falls · Congo River · World's greatest untapped hydropower · 40,000 MW potential
tagline: Africa does not lack energy. Africa lacks infrastructure. Build it.

roles.name: Solar Installer
roles.desc: Solar panel installation, micro-grid design, maintenance

roles.desc: Grid design, infrastructure, high-voltage systems

name: Transport Village
meaning: The Pearl of the Coast — greatest Swahili trading port, called 'most beautiful city in the world' by Ibn Battuta
history: Kilwa Kisiwani controlled the entire East African gold trade and was the wealthiest city in sub-Saharan Africa at its peak. Chinese porcelain, Indian cloth, Persian pottery, and Arabian glass have all been found in Kilwa's ruins. The Swahili navigators who built Kilwa had mastered monsoon winds centuries before European sailors understood them.
guardianDesc: Mami Wata is the pan-African water spirit worshipped from West Africa to East Africa to the Diaspora. Every journey begins with her blessing. She rules all waters between peoples and protects all who cross water for commerce.
origin: Greatest Swahili Coast port · Ibn Battuta: 'most beautiful city' · 1331 CE
tagline: Africa's navigators connected continents before Europe set sail.

roles.name: Road Driver
roles.desc: Long-haul trucking, intercity transport, goods delivery

roles.desc: Fleet management, route optimisation, supply chain

roles.name: Motorcycle Taxi (Boda)

roles.name: Fleet Manager
roles.desc: Vehicle fleet tracking, maintenance scheduling, cost control

name: Technology Village
nationFull: Egypt · Ptolemaic Alexandria · Library of the World
meaning: The Library of the World — 700,000 scrolls, where Eratosthenes, Hypatia, and Heron invented science
history: Eratosthenes calculated the Earth's circumference in 240 BC within 2% accuracy. Hypatia (360–415 AD) invented early astrolabe mathematics. Heron of Alexandria built the first steam engine in 10 AD. The library held 700,000 scrolls — the collected knowledge of the ancient world. Africa did not receive science from the world. Africa gave it.
guardianDesc: Thoth is the Ancient Egyptian god of science, mathematics, and knowledge systems. The divine engineer. The first coder. Original architect of all knowledge systems. All who store, process, and transmit information work under his gaze.
origin: Library of Alexandria · 700,000 scrolls · Eratosthenes · Hypatia · c. 285 BCE
tagline: Hypatia invented mathematics here. You are her heir.

roles.desc: Backend, frontend, fullstack development across platforms

name: Media Village
nationFull: Mali · City of Manuscripts and Griots
meaning: Where Stories Are Sacred — home to 700,000 manuscripts and the world's oldest oral media tradition
history: The Griot tradition is the world's oldest continuous media system — stretching back 3,000 years. Griots were simultaneously historians, journalists, musicians, diplomats, and archivists. The Kouyaté family of griots can recite 700 years of dynastic genealogy from memory. Today's viral content creators are their direct descendants.
guardianDesc: Kouyaté the Griot is the royal genealogist who can recite 700 years of dynasty from memory. The living newspaper. The walking archive. The voice that never dies. Every journalist and broadcaster is a Jeli in this tradition.
origin: City of 333 saints · 700,000 manuscripts · Capital of African knowledge · c. 1000 CE
tagline: The Griot is the centre of African media. Always was.

roles.name: Journalist
roles.desc: News reporting, investigative journalism, editorial

roles.name: Video Editor

roles.name: Ad Creative
roles.desc: Advertising, campaign creative, brand design

name: Finance Village
meaning: The Golden Gateway — all trans-Saharan gold passed through this Amazigh city for 600 years
history: Sijilmasa was the Amazigh-founded city at the northern terminus of the trans-Saharan gold trade. For 600 years, virtually all gold from sub-Saharan West Africa passed through Sijilmasa before reaching the Mediterranean. Its merchants developed letters of credit, currency exchange, and partnership contracts — anticipating modern banking by 800 years.
guardianDesc: Aje is the Yoruba goddess of wealth and marketplace fortune. She inhabits the marketplace and decides who prospers. She rewards those who treat money as a communal responsibility, not a personal hoard.
origin: Amazigh trading city · Northern gateway of trans-Saharan gold trade · c. 757 CE
guardianOrigin: Yoruba Pantheon · Nigeria · Goddess of Wealth
tagline: Africa invented international finance. Sijilmasa is the proof.

roles.name: Bank Officer
roles.desc: Retail banking, savings, loans, account management

roles.name: Insurance Agent

roles.desc: Digital assets, decentralised finance, web3

roles.name: Ajo Circle Keeper
roles.desc: Traditional savings circles, rotating credit, community funds

name: Justice Village
meaning: The Grass Courts — justice done face to face, seated in the grass. Rwanda processed 1.2 million cases with this tradition
history: Gacaca means 'justice on the grass' in Kinyarwanda. For 500 years communities gathered as equals — seated on the ground — to resolve disputes. After the 1994 genocide, Rwanda adapted this ancient system: between 2005–2012, over 12,000 Gacaca courts processed 1.2 million cases — the largest community justice initiative in human history.
guardianDesc: Imana is the supreme moral authority in Rwandan spiritual tradition who cannot be bribed. He does not sleep. He sees every act of justice and injustice equally. Justice Village answers to Imana's gaze.
origin: Rwanda's 500-year-old grass-roots justice tradition · 'justice on the grass'
tagline: Africa invented restorative justice. The world is still catching up.

roles.desc: Litigation, legal advice, contract law, corporate law

roles.name: Prison Reform Worker
roles.desc: Rehabilitation, reintegration, correctional advocacy

name: Government Village
meaning: The Throne of Thrones — one of the four great powers of the ancient world alongside Rome, Persia, and China
history: The Aksumite Empire issued its own gold coins — the only sub-Saharan African empire to do so in antiquity. When Persia sent an ambassador to Aksum, they were received as equals. Aksum built the tallest standing stone monuments of the ancient world — obelisks 24 metres tall. Ethiopia is the only African nation never fully colonised, rooted in this 2,000-year tradition of sovereign governance.
guardianDesc: Negus Negusti means 'King of Kings' — the title of every Aksumite emperor. Not a person but a principle: sovereign African governance that answers to no foreign power. The spirit that kept Ethiopia forever sovereign.
origin: One of the four great ancient world powers · Ethiopia · c. 100 CE · own gold coinage
tagline: Governance as sovereignty, not servitude. The Aksumite way.

roles.name: Civil Servant
roles.desc: Public administration, government operations, policy execution

roles.desc: Elected leadership, policy-making, constituency service

roles.name: Elections Officer

name: Security Village
meaning: The Ones Who Protect — world's only all-female professional army, 6,000 warriors who French Foreign Legion veterans called the fiercest they ever faced
history: The Agojie numbered 6,000 — one-third of the entire Dahomey military. They trained from age eight. When the French colonial army invaded in 1890, the Agojie led the resistance. French Foreign Legion veterans reported that the Agojie were the most ferocious fighters they had ever faced. They were never defeated in single combat.
guardianDesc: Ogun is the Yoruba/Fon god of iron, war, and protection. Worshipped from Nigeria to Cuba to Brazil to Haiti. The patron of soldiers, surgeons, and all who work with iron. He does not protect cowards. The Agojie invoked Ogun before every battle.
origin: All-female elite military regiment of Dahomey · World's only professional women's standing army
tagline: 400 years of Africa's most disciplined protection tradition.

roles.name: Police Officer
roles.desc: Law enforcement, community policing, crime prevention

roles.name: Cybersecurity Government
roles.desc: State cybersecurity, critical infrastructure protection

roles.name: Fire & Rescue

roles.desc: Personal protection, premises security, event security

roles.name: Community Watch

roles.name: Emergency Management
roles.desc: Disaster response, civil protection, emergency coordination

name: Spirituality Village
nationFull: Egypt · Ancient Thebes · Karnak Temple Complex
meaning: The Most Sacred of Places — largest ancient religious site on Earth, 2,000 years to build, 30 pharaohs contributed
history: Karnak was not one temple but a city of temples — the largest religious complex ever constructed. For 2,000 years each pharaoh added to it. Egyptian spiritual philosophy — Ma'at (truth, justice, harmony), the soul's journey, the afterlife — directly influenced Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. Africa did not receive spirituality from the world. Africa gave it.
guardianDesc: Ifa/Orunmila is the Yoruba oracle system — UNESCO declared it a Masterpiece of Oral Heritage. The 256 Odù form a body of oral literature larger than the Bible. Orunmila witnessed the creation of the world and knows every destiny that will unfold within it.
origin: Largest religious complex ever built · 2,000 years under construction · 30 pharaohs · c. 2055 BCE
guardianOrigin: Yoruba Pantheon · Nigeria — Pan-African Oracle System
tagline: Africa gave spirituality to the world. Karnak is the oldest proof.

roles.name: Islamic Scholar / Imam
roles.desc: Islamic leadership, teaching, community guidance

roles.name: Christian Minister
roles.desc: Church leadership, pastoral care, community service

roles.desc: Indigenous spiritual practice, ritual, ancestral communication

roles.desc: Spiritual guidance, life coaching, psychological-spiritual support

roles.name: Funeral Director
roles.desc: Death care, ceremony, grief support, body preparation

name: Fashion Village
nationFull: Nigeria · Nupe Kingdom · Bida City of Craftsmen
meaning: The City of Master Craftsmen — 1,000 years of Nupe brasswork, glassmaking, and handwoven cloth
history: The Nupe of Bida have produced Africa's finest brasswork, glassware, and handwoven textiles for over 1,000 years. Their glass beads were initially mistaken for Venetian manufacture by European traders. The Fashion Village draws from all of Africa: kente from Ghana, bogolan from Mali, adire from Yoruba Nigeria, shweshwe from South Africa, kitenge from East Africa, and haik from North Africa.
guardianDesc: Osun is the Yoruba goddess of beauty, gold, honey, rivers, and all beautiful things. Worshipped from Nigeria to Cuba to Brazil. Patron of fashion, jewellery, and everything that makes life beautiful rather than merely functional.
origin: Capital of Nupe Kingdom · 1,000 years of master glasswork, brasswork, weaving
guardianOrigin: Yoruba Pantheon · Nigeria · River Goddess of Beauty
tagline: 1,000 years of African luxury craft. Not borrowed — built.

roles.name: Fashion Designer
roles.desc: African fashion design, custom garments, collection development

roles.name: Jewellery Designer

name: Family Village
nationFull: South Africa · Zimbabwe · Nguni Ubuntu Philosophy
meaning: I Am Because We Are — Umuntu ngumuntu ngabantu. The philosophical spine of Southern African civilisation
history: Ubuntu has been the foundational operating system of Nguni family life for at least 1,000 years. Nelson Mandela built his post-apartheid reconciliation philosophy on it. The South African Constitution draws from it. Ubuntu is the original African answer to what a human being owes to another: everything. The family is not just blood — it is everyone who has ever shared your fire.
guardianDesc: Nana Buluku is the supreme androgynous creator in Fon/Ewe tradition — both mother and father, both ancestor and descendant. The guardian of every family circle that has ever existed. She created the universe and all the divine twins.
origin: 'Umuntu ngumuntu ngabantu' · A person is a person through other persons · Zulu/Xhosa/Ndebele
tagline: I am because we are. Ubuntu is the software Africa runs on.

roles.name: Family Elder
roles.desc: Community wisdom, elder mentorship, family leadership

roles.name: Marriage Coordinator

roles.name: Child Welfare Worker

roles.name: Adoption Officer

roles.name: Homemaker

roles.name: Youth Mentor
roles.desc: Intergenerational knowledge transfer, youth development

name: Hospitality Village
meaning: The Guest Is King — Wolof philosophy of radical hospitality. The guest must be fed before questions are asked
history: Teranga demands that a host give everything to a guest before taking care of themselves. This was not charity — it was sacred obligation, the bedrock of West African trading culture. Caravansaries on every trans-Saharan route operated on the Teranga principle: feed first, trade later. Teranga is why West African market cities became the most cosmopolitan places on medieval Earth.
guardianDesc: Osun is the Yoruba goddess of rivers and the open door. Her rivers flow to every village. No one who arrives at her banks leaves thirsty or hungry. The spirit of the welcome that costs the host everything and enriches both.
origin: Wolof philosophy of radical unconditional hospitality · 'The Land of Teranga'
guardianOrigin: Yoruba Pantheon · Nigeria · Goddess of Plenty
tagline: Feed first. Trade later. The most ancient customer service tradition.

name: Sports Village
meaning: The Running Nation — Kalenjin people represent 0.06% of world population but won 40% of international distance competitions (1980–2011)
history: Kalenjin-speaking people from Kenya's Rift Valley represent 0.06% of the world's population but won 40% of all international long-distance competitions between 1980 and 2011. Ancient Nandi warriors trained by running between villages — 50 miles a day was not unusual. Eliud Kipchoge, the first human to run a marathon under 2 hours, is Nandi/Kalenjin. Africa's body was made for this.
guardianDesc: Sango is the Yoruba/Fon god of thunder and explosive power. Worshipped from Nigeria to Cuba to Brazil to Trinidad. The patron of athletes and warriors. He strikes fast. He never misses. Lightning is Sango's sprint.
origin: Nandi/Kalenjin ethnic cluster · Rift Valley Kenya · Most Olympic gold per capita in distance running
tagline: The most dominant athletic tradition in human history lives here.

name: Holdings Village
nationFull: Eritrea · Aksumite Empire · Red Sea Gateway
meaning: The Eternal Gateway — crossroads of Africa, Arabia, India, Rome, and Persia. Every trade route passed through here
history: Adulis (modern Zula, Eritrea) was the principal Red Sea port of the Aksumite Empire. The Periplus of the Erythraean Sea (60 AD) describes it as a major trading port where African ivory, gold, and rhinoceros horn were exchanged for Indian cloth, Egyptian papyrus, Persian silver, and Arabian goods. Four continents connected through one port.
guardianDesc: Esu/Elegba is the keeper of all crossroads and doorways. Must be propitiated before any prayer reaches any other deity. Also called Legba (Fon), Papa Legba (Haitian Vodou). He is the first spirit and the last — the one who opens every door.
origin: Greatest Red Sea port of the ancient world · Gateway of the Aksumite Empire · c. 600 BCE
guardianOrigin: Yoruba Pantheon · Nigeria — Pan-African Crossroads God
tagline: Every journey begins at Adulis. Esu decides which path is yours.
